commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A maintainer security/compatibility decision and branch revision are needed because the proposed low-level clear is broader than a safe automated repair.; Needs attention: The diff changes a fail-closed runtime tool-policy guard into fail-open behavior for CLI backends.; Review finding: [P1] Keep explicit toolsAllow fail-closed — src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts:271 (sha=877f7d56df077fc551ea69208adbed414b266868)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes CLI runner preparation to warn and clear any runtime `toolsAllow` for CLI backends instead of throwing, and updates the unit test to expect success.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +1. Total +2 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main rejects any defined CLI runtime `toolsAllow`, and the cron caller already shows which auto-default values should be dropped versus which explicit restrictions should reach that guard.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer security/compatibility decision and branch revision are needed because the proposed low-level clear is broader than a safe automated repair.

target.title: fix(cli-backend): warn+ignore toolsAllow instead of hard-throw
target.body:
## What

Replace the hard `throw` for `params.toolsAllow !== undefined` in `prepareCliRunContext` with a `cliBackendLog.warn` + `params.toolsAllow = undefined`, so CLI-backend runs that carry a `toolsAllow` array proceed instead of crashing.

## Why

OpenClaw 2026.6.10 introduced a strict guard:

```
CLI backend claude-cli cannot enforce runtime toolsAllow; use an embedded runtime for restricted tool policy
```

CLI backends genuinely cannot enforce per-run tool restriction — that hasn't changed. But the hard throw broke **every** cron/agent run whose default model is a CLI backend (e.g. `claude-cli/claude-opus-4-8`), because those runs routinely carry a `toolsAllow` array in their params. Pre-2026.6.10 behavior silently ignored `toolsAllow` for CLI backends and the run proceeded normally.

The regression hit production immediately: disk-guard, daily-morning-brief, and OpenClaw daily health-check crons all entered a hard-fail loop (10–18 consecutive errors) with the exact error string above.

## Change

- `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts`: replace the `throw new Error(...)` with `cliBackendLog.warn(...)` (includes `session=${redactedSessionKey}` for traceability) + `params.toolsAllow = undefined` so the run proceeds with full backend tools.
- `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`: update the corresponding test from "expects rejection" to "expects resolve + toolsAllow cleared on context.params".

1-line behavior revert. No new interfaces, no API changes, no auth changes.

## Real behavior proof

**Problem**: every CLI-backend cron/run hard-fails with:
```
CLI backend claude-cli cannot enforce runtime toolsAllow; use an embedded runtime for restricted tool policy
```

**Environment**: OpenClaw `2026.6.10`, gateway running `claude-cli/claude-opus-4-8` as agent default model, crons with `toolsAllow` arrays in their runtime params.

**After applying warn+ignore patch + gateway restart**, three representative crons were force-run:

| Cron | Result | Duration | Pre-fix state |
|---|---|---|---|
| disk-guard | `ok` | 8.5s | failing |
| daily-morning-brief | `ok` | 154s (delivered with audio) | 10 consecutive errors |
| OpenClaw daily health-check | `ok` | 88s | 18 consecutive errors |

Zero `toolsAllow` errors after the fix. The warning log fires once per affected run, giving full visibility that tool restriction was requested but cannot be enforced.

## Test plan

- Updated unit test `warns and ignores toolsAllow for CLI backends (run proceeds with full backend tools)` in `prepare.test.ts` — asserts the call resolves and `context.params.toolsAllow` is `undefined`.
- Real behavior validated on live OpenClaw `2026.6.10` instance as above.
- `tsc --noEmit` on the affected files: no errors.

No `CHANGELOG.md` change (per CONTRIBUTING).
target.author: bitmapasset
